marketState: neutral-to-bearish, with a short-term oversold bounce risk inside a bearish structural backdrop
summary: Price sits -10.3pp below its seasonal median with low reliability (50pp spread) and a slightly negative typical 4-week seasonal drift, a soft bearish tilt. Storage printed a +72 Bcf bearish surprise and sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average, a genuinely bearish supply cushion heading into the back half of summer. Daily/weekly technicals are uniformly bearish (50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe, price closed below the lower daily Bollinger band) while 1h/4h RSI near 25-36 flag an oversold, bounce-prone short-term condition. Front price ($2.940) is essentially at model fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417), so there is no fair-value extension to trade off directly. Near-term weather is the one clearly bullish input (demand +13.1%/+11.1% vs 5y in the 1-7D and 8-16D windows, rangePos 0.69-0.83) even as it cools off 24h-over-24h; futures-curve data was unavailable this run, which caps confidence in the short-term call.

longTerm.entry: $2.98-$3.05 (sell into a bounce toward fair value)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise this week, and seasonality shows price already -10.3pp below its median with a negative typical 4-week move and only 31% odds of a higher price in 4 weeks (though reliability is flagged Low). Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only the 35th percentile of 5y range, leaving room to add rather than signaling a crowded/exhausted short.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained storage deficit surprise (builds running below forecast for 2+ weeks), a multi-week heat wave holding weather demand well above 5y normal, or price reclaiming and holding above the $3.20 area would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (counter-trend bounce, smaller size)
shortTerm.rationale: Both weather windows show demand well above 5-year norms (+13.1% 1-7D, +11.1% 8-16D) supporting price near-term, and 1h/4h RSI (35.7/24.9) plus price sitting below the lower daily Bollinger band point to an oversold bounce setup even though the daily/weekly trend label remains bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and daily close below $2.80, or weather demand rolling over toward/below the 5y average, would invalidate the bounce and favor resuming the downtrend.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a +72 Bcf bearish weekly surprise
Cooling-season weather demand running +11-13% above 5y norms in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows
Front price essentially at fair value ($2.940 vs $2.917 fair, band $2.417-$3.417)
Uniform bearish technical structure (50<200 EMA cross all timeframes) but oversold RSI on 1h/4h/1d
Managed-money net short but only 35th percentile of 5y range - not a crowded position
risks: Seasonality reliability is flagged Low (50pp spread), so the seasonal bearish tilt could easily fail to play out
Futures curve data was unavailable this run, removing a key short-term contango/backwardation check
A hotter-than-expected forecast shift or a bullish storage surprise could quickly overwhelm the bearish fundamental backdrop
